---
title: Government Health Spending Targets
format: html
---

Inspired by https://data.one.org/analysis/african_gov_health-spending-off-track

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(nanoparquet)

country <- read_parquet("country.parquet")
country_year <- read_parquet("country-year.parquet")
health_spending <- read_parquet("health_spending.parquet")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-gghed-targets
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
gghed <- health_spending |>
  filter(expenditure_type == "gghed", year == 2023) |>
  left_join(country, by = "iso3_code") |>
  left_join(country_year, by = c("iso3_code", "year")) |>
  mutate(
    usd_per_capita = usd2023 / population,
    pct_gdp = usd2023 / gdp_usd * 100
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(usd_per_capita), !is.na(pct_gdp), pct_gdp > 0) |>
  mutate(is_africa = region == "Sub-Saharan Africa")

ggplot(gghed, aes(usd_per_capita, pct_gdp, size = population)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 86, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  geom_point(
    data = \(d) filter(d, !is_africa),
    colour = "grey75",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = \(d) filter(d, is_africa),
    colour = "#4DBFCF",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 95,
    y = 15.5,
    label = "$86 per person target \u2192",
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.7,
    y = 5.3,
    label = "\u2191 5% of GDP target",
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_dollar()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 20, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Many **African countries** are far from reaching health spending targets",
    subtitle = "Domestic general government health expenditure",
    x = "US$ per person",
    y = "percent of GDP"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```
